title: Survivor Benefits for Military Spouses
summary: Military spouses may have access to specific survivor benefits beyond general Social Security, worth exploring directly.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Survivor Benefits for Military Spouses | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Military spouses may have access to specific survivor benefits beyond general Social Security, worth exploring directly.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation may apply for service-connected deaths.
The Survivor Benefit Plan, if elected during service, provides ongoing monthly payments.
Health care (TRICARE) eligibility may also change.

paragraphs: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation is a tax-free monthly benefit available to some surviving spouses and dependents when a death is connected to military service, distinct from general Social Security survivor benefits.

If the veteran elected the Survivor Benefit Plan during their service, it provides an ongoing monthly annuity to the surviving spouse — worth confirming whether this election was made, since it directly affects what's owed.

nextSteps: Ask the VA specifically about Dependency and Indemnity Compensation eligibility.
Check whether the Survivor Benefit Plan was elected.
Confirm any changes to TRICARE or other military health benefits.
disclaimer: General information — the VA or Defense Finance and Accounting Service can confirm specific benefit status.
